title: Sweat / Yellowing on Silk
timeEstimate: Pro-lane for set yellowing; one home pass max for fresh
stainChemistry: Sweat on silk has two halves: fresh salt and sweat residue (mostly water-soluble), and the cumulative aluminum from antiperspirant that builds up over time and turns yellow under heat. Vinegar is the right tool for fresh sweat on silk — silk-safe at low dilution and short dwell — but the hot-water plus alkaline plus enzyme cleaners that lift built-up yellow from cotton all damage silk. Enzyme cleaners would damage silk's own protein. Set yellow halos on silk are pro-only.
whyThisWorks: For fresh sweat: cold water plus a brief dab of diluted vinegar releases the residue without damaging the fiber. For set yellow halos, the pro adds cold detergent plus a silk-safe reducing bleach — chemistry home can't match. Home limit: one careful pass on fresh; set yellow goes to a specialist.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): sweat is salt + sweat proteins + urea, often plus aluminum from antiperspirant. Aluminum chlorohydrate forms an aluminum-protein complex that yellows under heat and locks to silk fibroin. Pro uses cold NSD + dilute acetic acid (60-sec dwell) + sodium hydrosulfite at controlled dilution for set yellow.
defaultAssumption: FRESH (today's wear) = one home pass with cold water + dilute vinegar. SET YELLOW (months of buildup, especially with heat exposure) = pro-only. Aluminum-free deodorant prevents recurrence on silk garments worth preserving.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Cold water through the BACK over a catch towel. Removes salt + fresh protein.
Cold NSD sponge press for residual protein. No enzymes — silk is protein.
Acid breaks aluminum-protein complex behind yellow halos AND restores silk's natural pH. STRICT 60-sec ceiling. Flush cold immediately.
For set yellow halos (cumulative aluminum-protein), reducing bleach is silk-safer than peroxide. Strict 60-sec dwell.
Cold final rinse. Press between dry towels, lay flat to air-dry.

steps: 5. Cold final rinse, press dry flat.
warnings: NEVER enzymes on silk — digests fibroin
NEVER hot water — sets aluminum-protein + denatures silk
NEVER hydrogen peroxide on silk — yellows dyes (worse than the stain)

homeSolutions.agent: Honest Answer — Fresh One Pass, Set Yellow Pro
homeSolutions.instruction: Sweat on silk has two cases. FRESH (today's wear, no yellow yet) = one careful home pass with cold water + dilute vinegar can handle it. SET YELLOW (months of underarm buildup, heat-set by dryer or iron) = pro-only. The aluminum-protein complex behind yellow halos doesn't lift with home chemistry on silk — peroxide yellows the silk worse than the stain, OxiClean weakens silk, hot water denatures fibroin. For valuable silk, prevention (aluminum-free deodorant) matters more than treatment.

homeSolutions.agent: Cold Water Back-Flush — Fresh Sweat Only
homeSolutions.instruction: Turn inside out. Place white towel under stain. Run COLD water through the BACK into the catch towel. Cold ONLY. Removes salt + fresh protein. Skip if the yellow is set — go to step 5.

Mix 1 part white vinegar with 1 part cool water. Press a cotton swab on the stain for 60 SECONDS MAX — set a timer. Flush with cool water immediately. Vinegar is the RIGHT tool for sweat-on-silk because it (a) breaks the aluminum-protein complex behind yellow, (b) restores silk's natural pH (~5), (c) is silk-safe at this dilution + dwell. Skip baking soda pairing — it neutralizes vinegar AND opens silk fiber.
Press between dry white towels. No wringing. Lay flat to air-dry, out of sun. NEVER hang wet silk. NEVER apply heat — even residual heat sets any remaining aluminum-protein into permanent yellow.

homeSolutions.agent: Set Yellow Goes to Pro and Aluminum-Free Deodorant Prevents Recurrence
homeSolutions.instruction: If yellow halos are already set (visible after wash + dry, or have been there for weeks), home chemistry can't safely lift them. Take to a silk specialist (call ahead). Tell them: "Cumulative sweat / antiperspirant yellowing on silk, age [weeks / months], one fresh round at home with cold + 60-sec vinegar. No enzymes, no peroxide, no heat." Pros use cold NSD + sodium hydrosulfite at controlled dilution. PREVENTION: switch to aluminum-free deodorant for silk garments you want to preserve. Stop using fabric softener (locks aluminum in).

steps: 1. Fresh sweat = one careful pass. Set yellow = pro-only.
2. Cold water back-flush from the reverse.
3. Dilute vinegar 60-sec ceiling — vinegar IS the right tool for fresh.
4. Press dry between towels, lay flat air-dry.
5. Set yellow / cumulative buildup = pro silk specialist.
6. Going forward: aluminum-free deodorant, no fabric softener.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop after one fresh-sweat pass. Set yellow on silk is pro-only — home chemistry yellows silk worse than the stain.
warnings: NEVER enzymes on silk — digests fibroin
NEVER hydrogen peroxide on silk — yellows dyes
NEVER bake silk in dryer with sweat — sets aluminum-protein permanently
Vinegar 60-sec ceiling — silk weakens with prolonged acid

neverDo: Never hot water on sweat-stained silk
Never dryer / iron heat on yellow silk
fiberSensitivities: Silk is protein fiber — same enzyme-digestion as sweat protein
Vinegar IS the right acid for sweat-on-silk at controlled dilution + dwell

materialWarnings: Vinegar is the right tool for fresh sweat on silk
Aluminum-free deodorant is the prevention path
Fabric softener locks deodorant residue into silk

escalation.when: Yellow halos are already set (visible after wash + dry), cumulative buildup has been heat-exposed (dryer / iron), or one fresh-sweat pass doesn't clear it.
escalation.whatToTell: "Sweat / antiperspirant yellowing on silk, age [today / weeks / months]. One fresh round at home with cold water + 60-sec dilute vinegar. No enzymes, no peroxide, no OxiClean, no heat. [Yellow halos visible / aluminum buildup suspected]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner specializing in silk (call ahead — confirm cold NSD + sodium hydrosulfite spotting capability for aluminum-protein removal)

contextQuestions: Fresh today, or yellow halos that have been there?
Has the silk been through a dryer / iron with sweat already?
Has fabric softener been used on this garment?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Aluminum-free deodorant doesn't cause this stain. Fabric softener + antiperspirant pair is the cumulative-yellow factory — switch one to break the cycle. Heat-set yellow halos are the hardest variant; pro success rate is moderate at best.
